ACME Corporation

Annual Report on Form 10-K for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2024.

Table of Contents

  • Item 1. Business
  • Item 1A. Risk Factors
  • Item 7. Management's Discussion

Item 1. Business

ACME Corporation designs and manufactures rocket-powered roller skates, portable holes, and assorted anvils. Our principal customers are coyotes operating in arid environments. We were incorporated in 1949.

Net revenue is concentrated among repeat customers. We expect revenue growth to track the population of road runners in the southwestern United States.

Item 1A. Risk Factors

Our products are subject to significant warranty claims arising from catastrophic failures during high-velocity pursuit scenarios. A single customer accounts for substantially all of our revenue, and the loss of this customer would materially harm our business.

Supply chain risk is elevated due to our reliance on a single Acme-brand spring manufacturer. We do not currently hedge against feather inflation.

Item 7. Management's Discussion and Analysis

Fiscal 2024 revenue was $42.0 million, up 12% from fiscal 2023. Gross margin expanded 180 basis points, primarily driven by lower TNT input costs and improved yield on our anvil casting line.

Operating expenses grew 8% year over year, reflecting investments in R&D for our next-generation jet-propelled boots program.
